top_five.rationale: True NBA for ~60% of MNC FMCG SG buyers — hire 5–60 in-house promoters on direct payroll. Beats agencies on perceived control; we beat it on retention, replacement and EMR-Intel tech.
Status-quo workflow at most SG distributors and indie brands — Excel + WhatsApp + a supervisor. Invisible competitor that wins by inertia; EMR-Intel field-force tech is the wedge.

Direct SG competitor — Singapore-incorporated FMCG promoter/merch agency with overlapping FairPrice/Watsons/Guardian roster. Same buyer, same channels, smaller bench; head-to-head deals every quarter.
SG-HQ retail-tech unicorn — Image Recognition + crowd-merch (Retail Watch) replaces field audits. Tier-1 logo overlap; threatens the EMR-Intel data layer above merch execution.
Smollan-CDM JV — global FMCG field-marketing incumbent with deep L'Oréal/Unilever/Reckitt MSAs in APAC. Threat is global-account capture; beatable via SG-local bench depth and price.

competitors.name: FMCG Link
target_market: FMCG brands selling into SG supermarkets, pharmacies and convenience
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; triangulated SGD 3,500–5,000/pax/mo for full-time merchandiser bundle (own salary + supervision + reporting)
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG-local FMCG merchandiser and promoter outsourcing with retail-channel coverage and routing reports.
strengths: Direct SG-incorporated entity with existing buyer relationships across FairPrice + Watsons + Guardian
Pure-play FMCG focus — not a generalist BTL agency taking merch as an add-on
Lower price point than tier-1 global agencies per buyer interviews 2026-Q1
weaknesses: No proprietary field-force SaaS — runs on Excel + WhatsApp + supervisor reports (verified from their website's lack of technology page, 2026-05)
Smaller bench than Elitez; cannot bid for 40–60 pax dedicated teams
No published retention metric or replacement guarantee — buyer-side risk during attrition spikes

competitors.name: SMT Retailtainment
target_market: FMCG brands needing in-store promoter sampling + activation in SG retail
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; mid-market SGD 4,000–5,500/pax/mo for promoter + supervisor stack (buyer-side interviews)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Retail activation, sampling and in-store promotion staffing for SG and MY FMCG brands.
strengths: Activation-and-event muscle — covers atrium roadshows that pure merch agencies decline
SG + MY presence allows cross-border activation continuity for regional brand managers
Direct relationships with mall management for atrium space buying
weaknesses: Activation bias — dedicated full-time merch teams are not their core motion
No field-force SaaS; reporting cadence is weekly Excel deck per founder interview Mar 2026
Pricing perceived as higher than pure-merch agencies because activation overhead is baked in

competitors.name: Promo Group Singapore
target_market: F&B and beauty FMCG brands needing in-store activation + promoter staffing
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; SGD 3,800–4,500/pax/mo per buyer triangulation
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG in-store promoter outsourcing and sampling for F&B and beauty FMCG.
strengths: Tight bench of trained F&B sampling promoters for FairPrice and Cold Storage
Same-week deployment capability for short campaigns
Lower overhead — owner-operated, can flex price
weaknesses: Owner-dependent operation; key-person risk per ACRA filing (single director)
No field-force tech moat — Excel + WhatsApp
Limited bench depth — cannot scale beyond ~30 pax simultaneous

competitors.name: First Alverstone
target_market: FMCG brands needing field merchandising + auxiliary supervision in SG
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; mid-market SGD 4,000–5,000/pax/mo per buyer interviews
competitors.primary_value_prop: Field merchandising and retail execution services for FMCG brands in SG and MY.
strengths: SG + MY footprint allows brand-side coordination across both markets through one vendor
Generalist merch + auxiliary staffing flexibility
Mid-tier price floor
weaknesses: Brand profile lower than tier-1 SG agencies; harder to win MNC procurement
No proprietary field-force app — reporting cadence is weekly
Limited published case studies on website (2026-05 audit)

competitors.name: Asia Outsourcing Services (AOS)
target_market: FMCG + retail brands needing outsourced merchandising and event staff in SG
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; SGD 3,800–4,500/pax/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: Outsourced manpower for SG FMCG merchandising, retail event staffing and promoter campaigns.
strengths: Long-standing SG entity (15+ years per ACRA filing)
Generalist outsourced-manpower play — flex bench across categories
Established relationships with SG retail chains
weaknesses: Generalist outsourcing — FMCG merchandising is one of many lines (HR services, payroll, event staffing)
No category-specific tech — same Excel + WhatsApp stack
Bench is not FMCG-trained at scale — turnover impact

competitors.name: RSG Retail Solutions
target_market: FMCG brands needing dedicated merchandising teams in SG supermarkets and pharmacies
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; SGD 4,000–4,800/pax/mo per buyer interviews
competitors.primary_value_prop: Dedicated FMCG merchandising teams scoped per brand with SG retail-chain coverage.
strengths: Dedicated-model focus, similar to Elitez's flagship product
Mid-sized SG bench depth
Stable client base in personal care segment
weaknesses: No proprietary tech stack — losing larger MNC procurement evaluations on tech criterion
Smaller total bench than Elitez 350+ ceiling
No published retention metric

competitors.name: Shopper Singapore
target_market: FMCG + retail brands needing shopper-marketing execution and field-force in SG
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; SGD 4,200–5,200/pax/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: Shopper marketing and in-store execution services for SG FMCG brands.
strengths: Strategy-led positioning beyond pure execution — sells shopper insights
Strong shopper-marketing branding for procurement decks
Cross-channel execution capability
weaknesses: Strategy add-ons inflate pricing relative to execution-only agencies
Limited tech moat — reporting still Excel/PowerPoint
Smaller execution bench than tier-1 agencies

competitors.name: CDM Smollan Asia
target_market: Global MNC FMCG (Unilever, P&G, Reckitt, L'Oréal) across SG, MY, ID, TH, VN, PH
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; SGD 5,000–6,500/pax/mo for full-service dedicated team (buyer interviews + GeBIZ historical FMCG-adjacent tenders)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Global FMCG field-marketing and retail execution incumbent serving MNC brands across APAC and SEA.
features: Dedicated merch teams at scale (50+ pax)
Proprietary FieldX field-force app (per Smollan investor materials)
strengths: Global master-services agreements with Unilever, P&G, Reckitt — locks them into our key target accounts
60,000+ staff globally; APAC bench depth (Smollan corporate 2024 disclosure)
Proprietary tech stack (FieldX) — credible alternative to brand asking 'do you have a system?'
weaknesses: Tier-1 pricing premium (~20–30% above SG-local agencies) absorbed by global MSA but visible in local renewals
Slower scope-change cycle — global MSA contracts route through regional layers
SG team is part of regional pool, not 100% SG-dedicated bench

competitors.name: DKSH Smollan (JV)
target_market: MNC FMCG brands wanting integrated distribution + merchandising across Asia
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; SGD 5,000–7,000/pax/mo equivalent (bundled with distribution; buyer interviews)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Integrated market expansion services — distribution + merchandising + brand activation across Asia.
strengths: Distribution + merch bundling lock-in — once a brand uses DKSH for distribution, switching merch alone is costly
Asia-wide footprint per DKSH 2024 annual report (350+ business units)
Strong technology investment (Smollan JV for field-force layer)
weaknesses: Bundle premium — brands paying for distribution overhead they may not need on merch-only contracts
Less flexible on shared/auxiliary models because tied to distribution route plans
Decision-making slower due to multi-country JV governance

competitors.name: Luen Thai Marketing Services
target_market: FMCG brands needing field merchandising across SG, MY and HK
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; SGD 4,500–5,500/pax/mo (buyer interviews)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Asia regional FMCG merchandising operator with HK heritage and SG + MY operating footprint.
strengths: HK + SG dual-base coordinates Greater Bay + SG MNC accounts seamlessly
Established roster of personal-care MNC clients
Long-tenured supervisors with low attrition
weaknesses: SG bench smaller than market-leading SG-native agencies
Pricing in mid-premium band, no PSG eligibility
Less aggressive on tech stack vs Smollan-class incumbents

competitors.name: Pico Global (Activation)
target_market: MNC brands needing experiential activation and trade-marketing events across Asia
competitors.pricing_range_published: Project-based; activation contracts SGD 50,000+ per campaign (industry reports)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Experiential brand activation, exhibitions and trade-marketing events at Asia regional scale.
strengths: Listed HK incumbent (HKSE: 0752); 40+ years; staff base 2,500+ globally
Production capability beyond merch — booth build, AV, exhibition logistics
Brand recognition at MNC procurement
weaknesses: Activation/exhibition focus — sustained dedicated merch is adjacent not core
Project pricing not per-pax/month; harder to compare on FTE basis
SG presence smaller than HK/CN base

competitors.name: Edelweiss BTL Marketing
target_market: FMCG + lifestyle brands needing BTL activation and in-store sampling
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; SGD 4,500–5,500/pax/mo activation-led
competitors.primary_value_prop: Below-the-line marketing activation, sampling and promoter staffing for FMCG in SG and MY.
strengths: BTL-credentialed roster including beauty and F&B portfolios
Creative + execution capability under one roof
Cross-border SG + MY
weaknesses: Activation-heavy positioning; not primarily a sustained-merch bench
No proprietary field-force app
Premium relative to pure-merch agencies

competitors.name: Edge Marketing
target_market: Beauty + lifestyle brands needing trade marketing + activation in SG
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; activation projects SGD 30k+; per-pax SGD 4,500/mo equivalent
competitors.primary_value_prop: Trade marketing and retail activation for beauty and lifestyle brands in Singapore.
strengths: Strong beauty-vertical relationships in SG department-store and pharmacy channels
Creative VM + retail design in-house
Boutique service positioning
weaknesses: Beauty-niche depth but limited scale into other FMCG categories
Activation-led, not dedicated-merch-led
No field-force SaaS

competitors.name: Primary Connect Asia
target_market: FMCG brands needing field merchandising across MY and SG
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; MY-anchored pricing imported; SGD 3,500–4,300/pax/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: Field merchandising and trade-execution agency operating across Malaysia and Singapore.
strengths: MY-anchored cost base = price-competitive on SG bench for buyers with mixed SG+MY needs
Established MY hypermarket relationships extend into SG via consolidation
Owner-operator agility on pricing
weaknesses: SG operating presence thinner than SG-native agencies
Brand recognition in SG procurement lower
No field-force tech moat

competitors.name: Marketing Alliance Malaysia
target_market: MNC FMCG needing MY field merchandising and SG cross-border execution
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; SGD 3,500–4,200/pax/mo for SG-deployed bench
competitors.primary_value_prop: Full-service trade marketing and merchandising across Malaysia with SG project capability.
strengths: Long-standing MY incumbent with established trade-marketing brand
Lower-cost MY base allows SG cross-border competitive pricing
Strong personal-care + F&B vertical depth
weaknesses: SG bench thin — bid-only model for SG-dedicated teams
No proprietary field-force tech
Limited SG case studies

competitors.name: PT Pejagan Brand Services
target_market: FMCG brands deploying field force across ID modern trade and general trade
competitors.pricing_range_published: ID-only pricing; ~IDR 7-9M/pax/month (~SGD 600–800/pax)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Indonesia field-marketing and merchandising for FMCG modern + general trade channels.
strengths: ID-domestic scale advantage (Indonesia is ~5x SG retail outlets)
Investment in field-force tech to manage scale
Strong relationships with national modern-trade chains
weaknesses: ID-only presence; not relevant to SG-primary buyers unless they expand
Lower price tolerance means lower investment in retention
Indonesia macro/forex volatility

competitors.name: Smartfield Indonesia
target_market: FMCG MNCs deploying merch teams across ID modern + general trade
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; ID local rates IDR 7-10M/pax/mo (~SGD 600–850)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Indonesia field-force outsourcing for FMCG MNCs with proprietary tech stack.
strengths: Tech-forward positioning vs ID legacy agencies
Recent funding round (2024 per TechInAsia) suggests growth posture
Strong MNC roster in personal care + beverage
weaknesses: ID-only; regional expansion still nascent
Tech stack proprietary but unproven outside ID
Foreign-entity sales motion thin for SG MNC buyers

competitors.name: Spire Research & Consulting (Trade Marketing Practice)
target_market: MNCs needing strategic trade-marketing consulting + lightweight in-store audits across Asia
competitors.pricing_range_published: Project-based, SGD 30k+ per audit engagement
competitors.primary_value_prop: Pan-Asian market intelligence and trade-marketing consulting with retail-audit capability.
strengths: Pan-Asia footprint with senior consulting bench
Buyer-side trust from years of category research
Cross-border audits at scale
weaknesses: Consulting + sample audits not sustained execution
Not field-force at scale
Pricing structured per project, not retainer

competitors.name: Trax Retail
target_market: Global CPG brands and retailers wanting AI-powered shelf intelligence, crowd-sourced merch and image-recognition audits
competitors.pricing_range_published: Enterprise SaaS — annual contracts USD 50k–500k per brand-country (G2 + Crunchbase + buyer interviews)
competitors.primary_value_prop: AI image recognition + crowd-sourced field force replacing/augmenting traditional merchandiser audits.
strengths: AI/CV moat — replaces hours of human field-force audit with photos + ML (per Trax 2024 product disclosures)
Tier-1 logo roster: Coca-Cola, Nestle, Henkel, Heineken — overlaps with our buyers (Trax 2024 customer page)
USD 1B+ valuation per latest Crunchbase, well-capitalised
weaknesses: Pure SaaS — no physical merch execution. Brands still need a field-force agency
Image recognition does not replace promoter sampling or NPI launch coverage
Enterprise pricing locks out mid-market FMCG

competitors.name: ShelfPerfect
target_market: FMCG brands and field agencies wanting image-recognition shelf-audit SaaS in SEA
competitors.pricing_range_published: Enterprise SaaS — SGD 1,500–5,000/mo per brand depending on SKU count (TechInAsia 2024)
competitors.primary_value_prop: SEA-built image-recognition shelf analytics SaaS for FMCG brands and field agencies.
strengths: SEA-built, SEA-priced (10-20% of Trax cost per buyer interviews)
Mobile-first; easy to deploy on agency field forces (vs requiring brand to overhaul stack)
Local support in EN/BM
weaknesses: Tech-only — does not staff merchandisers; needs agency partner
Smaller customer base than Trax
Bootstrapped — slower roadmap on advanced CV models

competitors.name: NielsenIQ Byzzer / Field Services
target_market: CPG brands wanting retail-measurement data, panel data, and field audits
competitors.pricing_range_published: Enterprise contracts USD 100k+/year per category; field services bundled
competitors.primary_value_prop: Global retail-measurement and panel-data provider with auxiliary field-audit services.
strengths: Category-share gold standard — most MNC brand managers built their thinking on NielsenIQ data
Pan-SEA panel network
Brand recognition at CPG procurement
weaknesses: Measurement-first, not execution — field audits are auxiliary
Pricing locks out non-MNC mid-market
Less day-of-execution agility

competitors.name: Circana (IRI + NPD)
target_market: CPG MNCs wanting retail-measurement and shopper analytics across global markets
competitors.pricing_range_published: Enterprise USD 80k+/year per category
competitors.primary_value_prop: Retail-measurement and shopper analytics platform for CPG manufacturers globally.
strengths: Direct NielsenIQ alternative with strong US and EU bench
Combined IRI + NPD scale (Circana 2024 merger)
Established APAC presence in beauty + F&B
weaknesses: Data layer, not field-force execution
SEA presence thinner than NielsenIQ
Enterprise-only price band

competitors.name: Repsly
target_market: CPG brands and field-marketing teams wanting cloud retail-execution software
competitors.pricing_range_published: USD 49–99/user/mo published on pricing page; SG buyers via reseller
competitors.primary_value_prop: Cloud retail-execution SaaS — visits, photos, audits, KPIs for field-force teams.
strengths: Published per-user pricing (USD 49–99) — lowest-friction entry point in category
Strong North America + EU footprint
G2 review density (700+ reviews) signals trust
weaknesses: No SG entity or named SG support — buyers escalate to US-time desks
Tooling only — no field-force bench
Not on PSG list; SG SMEs pay headline USD

competitors.name: Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud
target_market: Enterprise CPG MNCs wanting retail-execution + DSD + trade-promo mgmt on Salesforce stack
competitors.pricing_range_published: USD 150–250/user/mo per pricing tier; enterprise volume
competitors.primary_value_prop: Enterprise retail-execution module on Salesforce — direct store delivery, visit planning, trade-promo management.
strengths: Already-deployed Salesforce stack at most MNC FMCG brands — switching cost favours them
Tableau analytics depth
Enterprise SLAs and support
weaknesses: Enterprise price point excludes SG mid-market
Implementation 6–12 months — not field-deployable quickly
Software-only — needs agency to staff the field

competitors.name: SAP Retail Execution
target_market: Enterprise CPG MNCs on SAP ECC/S4HANA wanting retail-execution module
competitors.pricing_range_published: Bundled with SAP CRM licensing; enterprise
competitors.primary_value_prop: Enterprise retail-execution module on SAP CRM for FMCG MNCs already on SAP stack.
strengths: Lock-in with SAP-running enterprises
ERP-grade audit trails
Cross-border consolidation
weaknesses: Heavy enterprise implementation
Mobile UX dated per Gartner 2024 review
Not field-deployable without major SI engagement

competitors.name: FairPrice Group Internal Merchandising
target_market: FairPrice house-brand and partner brands wanting in-house FairPrice-employed merchandisers
competitors.pricing_range_published: Internal cost recovery; not market priced
competitors.primary_value_prop: FairPrice internal field-merchandising headcount serving house-brand and select partner brands.
strengths: Inside the retailer — fastest planogram and replenishment access
Direct accountability to FairPrice category managers
weaknesses: Cannot serve competitor brands in same category — conflict of interest
Limited scope: covers house-brand priorities first
Outsourcing to FairPrice ties brand strategy to retailer

competitors.name: DIY — In-house FMCG promoter team (brand-employed)
target_market: MNC FMCG brand managers preferring to employ promoters directly on brand HRIS
competitors.pricing_range_published: Fully loaded SGD 3,000–3,600/pax/mo (basic salary ~SGD 2,200–2,600 + CPF 17% + medical/insurance + leave + supervisor cost amortised)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Brand employs its own field merchandisers on its HRIS — maximum control, full HR burden.
strengths: Maximum control over brand experience in-store
No agency margin
Direct line of sight on performance
weaknesses: Full HR burden — recruiting, payroll, attrition replacement, MOM/CPF compliance
Attrition during peak (CNY/Christmas) is brand's problem — no free replacement
No technology layer unless brand builds or licenses one — most run Excel + WhatsApp
Hidden cost: brand HR overhead ~10–15% loaded into the manpower cost

implications.headline: DIY in-house is the silent NBA — every Elitez deal must explicitly displace it
implications.body: MNCs default to direct-employment merch teams because of perceived control. Our pitch must explicitly cost-compare loaded HR overhead (SGD 3,300/pax loaded) vs Elitez SGD 4,500/pax with free replacement + EMR-Intel + supervisor; the SGD 1,200/pax delta buys away the attrition tail risk.

competitors.name: DIY — Excel + WhatsApp + Supervisor
target_market: Distributors, indie FMCG brands, and budget-constrained mid-market managing field force on spreadsheets
competitors.pricing_range_published: Negligible tooling cost; SGD 200/mo for Microsoft 365 + supervisor mobile phones
competitors.primary_value_prop: Manage field merchandisers with a daily Excel report, a WhatsApp group, and a senior supervisor doing spot-checks.
strengths: Zero software cost
Familiar tools — no onboarding friction
Flexible — no vendor lock-in
weaknesses: Zero visibility for brand HQ between supervisor reports
No structured planogram/MSL data — impossible to benchmark share-of-shelf objectively
Photo evidence scattered across WhatsApp threads — no audit trail
Attrition + scope drift invisible until it's a crisis

competitors.name: DIY — Big global agency (Acosta / Crossmark) via SG broker
target_market: MNCs trying to extend a global Acosta/Crossmark MSA via SG broker partner
competitors.pricing_range_published: Indirect via SG broker; SGD 5,500–7,500/pax/mo loaded incl. broker margin (industry estimate)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Extend a US/UK global retail-execution MSA into SG via local broker — keeps reporting consistent at HQ.
strengths: HQ procurement comfort — single global vendor on paper
Reporting consistency across markets
weaknesses: Two layers of margin (Acosta + SG broker) inflate price ~20–30% vs SG-direct
SG broker has no employment of the field force — accountability gap
No PSG eligibility for SG SME — pure premium spend
SLA and replacement run through US/UK time zones

competitors.name: Accenture Song — Trade Marketing Transformation
target_market: MNC CMOs/CSOs wanting strategy + tech transformation of trade-marketing function
competitors.pricing_range_published: Engagement-based SGD 500k–5M per multi-month transformation project (GeBIZ historical Accenture awards)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Strategy-led transformation of trade-marketing and retail-execution function with tech build-out.
strengths: Tier-1 strategy brand at CMO/CSO level
Global delivery muscle
Cross-functional capability
weaknesses: Strategy-and-tech, not bench — no field force
Project cost orders of magnitude above pure-merch agencies
Implementation 12–24 months — wrong cycle time for in-quarter merch needs

competitors.name: Deloitte Consumer Industry SEA
target_market: MNC FMCG CMOs and CSOs wanting consulting-led retail-execution strategy + tech advisory
competitors.pricing_range_published: Engagement-based SGD 300k–3M (Deloitte SG public sector GeBIZ awards as proxy)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Consumer-industry strategy and tech advisory including trade-marketing and retail-execution roadmaps.
strengths: Big-4 brand at C-suite level
Consumer industry depth
Cross-border SEA muscle
weaknesses: Advisory, not execution
Slow cycle time
Expensive vs in-store outcome delivered

competitors.name: NCS Retail / Smart-Field Practice
target_market: Retailer-led FMCG digital transformation; SG retail chains and house-brand operations
competitors.pricing_range_published: Engagement-based; SG public-sector contracts SGD 500k–10M (GeBIZ)
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG-local systems-integrator with retail and consumer-goods digital practice.
strengths: SG-local SI with WSG/IMDA recognised standing
Retailer-side relationships (FairPrice etc.)
Deep tech bench
weaknesses: Brand-side retail execution is not core; mostly retailer-side IT
Long delivery cycle
Not a manpower-bench play

competitors.name: Freelance supervisor + gig-platform merchandisers
target_market: Indie SG brands and short-burst campaigns wanting gig-style merchandisers via FastJobs/Workclass/PartTimeJobs
competitors.pricing_range_published: SGD 12–18/hr gig rate × ~200 hrs/mo ≈ SGD 2,400–3,600/pax/mo unsupervised; +SGD 600–900 freelance supervisor
competitors.primary_value_prop: Hire promoters per-shift through gig platforms with a freelance supervisor stitching them together.
strengths: Lowest unit cost when activity is intermittent
Zero bench-carrying overhead
Very fast ramp-up for short campaigns
weaknesses: Quality variance — promoters rotate per shift
No brand familiarity build-up
Supervisor coordination by WhatsApp only
Attrition during high-demand periods is total

competitors.name: iPromote
target_market: SG F&B and household-care brands needing promoter staffing and sampling
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; SGD 3,800–4,500/pax/mo (buyer interviews)
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG-local promoter staffing for F&B and household-care brands across supermarket and pharmacy channels.
strengths: Owner-operator agility on price
Strong household-care vertical relationships
Quick same-week ramp
weaknesses: Key-person risk
Limited tech
Bench depth caps at ~25 pax simultaneous

competitors.name: ScanBuy Marketing Services
target_market: FMCG brands needing in-store data + merchandising in MY with SG extension
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; MY-anchored ~SGD 3,800–4,300/pax/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: MY-anchored field-merchandising and in-store data services with SG project capability.
strengths: Data-led positioning vs pure-execution agencies
MY cost base = competitive SG bid
Good MY hypermarket relationships (Aeon, Tesco-equivalents)
weaknesses: SG presence shallow
Limited SG entity infrastructure
No PSG eligibility

competitors.name: Kantar Worldpanel SEA
target_market: FMCG MNCs wanting purchase-panel data, brand insights and retail audits
competitors.pricing_range_published: Enterprise USD 60–200k/year per category
competitors.primary_value_prop: Global consumer-panel and FMCG market-insights provider with retail-audit capability.
strengths: Established panel depth across SEA
Trusted at MNC procurement
Cross-market consolidation
weaknesses: Measurement-first, not execution
Premium price band
Cycle time slow for in-quarter merch needs

competitors.name: Concept Brand Activation
target_market: FMCG and lifestyle brands needing in-store activation and merch combined
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; SGD 4,000–4,800/pax/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG in-store activation and merchandising combined under one roster.
strengths: Combined activation + merch sale to brand manager (single PO)
Strong relationships in beverage and snack categories
Quick ramp
weaknesses: Project-style billing harder to compare to monthly retainers
No proprietary tech
Limited published case studies (2026-05 audit)

competitors.name: FMCG Works Vietnam
target_market: FMCG MNCs deploying field force in VN modern + general trade
competitors.pricing_range_published: VN-only; ~VND 12–18M/pax/mo (~SGD 600–800)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Vietnam field-merchandising and trade-marketing execution agency for FMCG MNCs.
strengths: VN-domestic scale advantage
Tech investment in field-force app
Strong relationships with Aeon, Vinmart, Co.opmart
weaknesses: VN-only; not relevant to SG buyers directly
Cross-border ops weak
Lower retention because of VN labor market churn

competitors.name: Shopper Intelligence Philippines
target_market: FMCG MNCs deploying field force in PH modern + sari-sari trade
competitors.pricing_range_published: PH local; ~PHP 25-35k/pax/mo (~SGD 600–800)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Philippines field-marketing and merchandising agency for FMCG MNCs across modern and sari-sari trade.
strengths: Sari-sari channel depth — PH-specific moat
Established Unilever/P&G relationships
Strong attrition management
weaknesses: PH-only — not SG-relevant
Less tech investment than ID peers
Cross-border ops thin

competitors.name: Thai Field Marketing Co.
target_market: FMCG brands wanting field merchandising across TH modern + traditional trade
competitors.pricing_range_published: TH local; ~THB 22-30k/pax/mo (~SGD 800–1,100)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Thailand FMCG field-marketing and merchandising operator across modern and traditional trade.
strengths: TH-domestic depth in beverage and snacks
Strong CP/Big-C/Tesco-equivalents relationships
Cost-base advantage
weaknesses: TH-only
No SG entity
Limited tech investment

competitors.name: Engage Marketing Services
target_market: FMCG + beauty brands needing combined trade-marketing + merch in SG
competitors.pricing_range_published: Not published; SGD 4,200–4,800/pax/mo (buyer interviews)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Trade-marketing and merchandising bundled for SG FMCG and beauty brands.
strengths: Mid-sized SG bench with capacity for 30+ pax campaigns
Trade-marketing + merch combined sale
BI partner story (vs pure Excel)
weaknesses: Field-force app outsourced — no proprietary stack
Smaller bench than Elitez ceiling
Limited published retention metric

competitors.name: IPG Mediabrands Trade SEA
target_market: MNC FMCG wanting integrated media + trade activation across SEA
competitors.pricing_range_published: Project-based; integrated with media spend
competitors.primary_value_prop: Media-network trade and shopper-marketing arm offering activation linked to media spend.
strengths: Media-tied trade dollars give holistic ROI story

weaknesses: Activation-led, not sustained merch
